##NAMES=Merlinoite
##RRUFFID=R130095
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=K_5_Ca_2_(Si_23_Al_9_)O_64_·24H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Pollena quarry, Vesuvius, Napoli, Campania, Italy
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Donald Doell
##DESCRIPTION=Sprays and clusters of colorless prismatic crystals associated with calcite. Merlinoite crystals are twinned with 90 deg rotations around c-axis.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R130095
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 14.2293 b: 14.233 c: 9.966 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 2018.2 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
